metadata.name: Docker Volume Restore
metadata.description: One-shot Bash restore script for extracting a .tar.gz container-path backup back into a Docker container through a helper container with optional stop/start, wipe-target, confirmation, and dry-run controls.
metadata.guide:
## Overview
This template renders a standalone `main.sh` restore script for Docker volume or bind-mounted data paths.

## What It Does
- Validates Docker, the target container, archive format, helper image, and target path before extracting data.
- Uses a temporary helper container with `--volumes-from` so the restore writes through the same mounts as the application container.
- Supports optional stop/start, optional wipe of the target path before extraction, interactive confirmation, `--yes`, and `--dry-run`.

## Prerequisites
- The archive should be a `.tar.gz` or `.tgz` created from the target container path, ideally by the complementary backup template.
- The helper image must be available locally or pullable by the Docker host.
- If you stop the application container for consistency, make sure the service can tolerate the maintenance window.

## Run
1. Render the template and confirm the container name, archive path, target path, and safety defaults.
2. Run `bash main.sh --help` to review flags.
3. Execute the restore with prompts enabled or pass `--yes` for automation.

## Notes
- Archives created by this repo preserve the full container path. The target path should usually match the original backed-up path.
- `--dry-run` prints the actions that would run without changing the target container data.

## Docs
- [Docker Volumes](https://docs.docker.com/storage/volumes/)
- [tar Manual](https://www.gnu.org/software/tar/manual/tar.html)

items.name: container_name
items.title: Container Name
items.description: Name of the target Docker container.

items.name: archive_path
items.title: Archive Path
items.description: Absolute path to the .tar.gz or .tgz archive on the Docker host.

items.name: restore_container_path
items.title: Container Path
items.description: Container path that should receive the restored files.

items.name: helper_image
items.title: Helper Image
items.description: Temporary helper image used to extract the archive through the target container mounts.

items.name: stop_container_during_restore
items.title: Stop Container
items.description: Stop the target container before restore if it is currently running.

items.name: start_container_after_restore
items.title: Restart After Restore
items.description: Start the container again after restore if this script stopped it.

items.name: wipe_target_path
items.title: Wipe Target Path
items.description: Delete existing files under the target path before extraction.

items.name: dry_run
items.title: Dry Run
items.description: Print planned actions without changing the target.
